data.title: Kuande Investments responds to former employee's comments: the content is untrue
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: A piece of news about "an employee of the well-known quantitative private equity firm Kuande Investment pointed out on a social platform that the company used brain-machine weapons to persecute company employees" continues to ferment in the private equity circle. As for the above "abstract" "accusations", Kuande Investment responded on February 18: "The company has noticed the inappropriate remarks about the company on the online platform. After verification, the publisher of the remarks did work for the company for a short period of time (joined the company in July 2022, and voluntarily resigned in early September of the same year, with a term of about 1 month). At present, the publisher has terminated the labor relationship with the company, and his remarks are personal behavior, and the content contains false and misleading information." (China Securities Taurus) 
Previously, a programmer named "hulezhi" sent 500 ETH to the black hole address to make a call on the chain, involving the boss of Kuande Investment Company using brain-machine weapons to persecute employees.

data.title: Controversy Surrounds Allegations Against Quantitative Private Equity Firm
data.description: According to Odaily, a controversy has emerged in the private equity sector following allegations made by a former employee of the well-known quantitative private equity firm, Kuande Investment. The employee claimed on social media that the company used 'brain-machine weapons' to persecute its staff. In response to these abstract accusations, Kuande Investment issued a statement on February 18, clarifying the situation. The company acknowledged the presence of inappropriate remarks about them on online platforms. They confirmed that the individual who made these statements was briefly employed by the company from July 2022 and voluntarily left in early September of the same year, serving for approximately one month. Kuande Investment emphasized that the individual is no longer associated with the company and that the statements made are personal opinions containing false and misleading information.

data.title: Solana Network Sees Significant Decline In Active Addresses
data.description: According to Odaily, blockchain analyst Ali reported on X that the number of active addresses on the Solana network has decreased significantly. The figures have dropped from 18.5 million in November last year to less than 8.4 million currently. This represents a decline of over 54% in the past four months.

data.title: Bitcoin realized and implied volatility near multi-year lows
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Glassnode data, BTC's two-week actual volatility has dropped to 32% annualized, one of the lowest levels in many years. In addition, the one-month volatility implied by options (that is, the market's expectation of four-week volatility) has fallen below 50% annualized, also hitting one of the lowest levels in many years. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Omni Network Integrates Superstate Tokenized U.S. Treasury Bond Product USTB to Optimize Financial Strategy
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Omni Network announced a partnership with Superstate, planning to use Superstate's short-term U.S. government securities fund USTB as part of its fund management strategy.
Omni Network will use USTB to expand its balance sheet, helping the team optimize its fund management strategy "while maintaining liquidity and flexibility."
USTB is currently a $274 million tokenized treasury product that provides investment exposure to short-term U.S. Treasuries. The fund allows holders to effectively manage capital and deploy idle assets to earn income.

data.title: BNB Drops Below 660 USDT with a 2.02% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 18, 2025, 05:12 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 660 USDT and is now trading at 659.450012 USDT, with a narrowed 2.02% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: OpenAI Considers Special Voting Rights To Counter Hostile Bids
data.description: According to Odaily, OpenAI is contemplating granting special voting rights to its nonprofit board to fend off hostile takeover attempts, including those from Elon Musk. The organization is considering this move to maintain board authority following its transition to a more traditional for-profit structure. CEO Sam Altman and other board members are evaluating new governance mechanisms to ensure control over the restructured company. This would enable OpenAI to counteract other investors, such as Microsoft and SoftBank, who are current supporters. Although no final decision has been made, the special voting rights would help protect OpenAI from hostile acquisitions by external parties like Musk. Previously, Musk made a $97.4 billion cash offer to acquire assets held by the nonprofit, including a controlling stake in its for-profit subsidiary.

data.title: Grok Voice Assistant Set for User Release in Coming Weeks
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on February 18, an engineer from xAI announced that the Grok voice assistant is currently undergoing improvements. The enhanced version is expected to be available to users in the coming weeks.
